# Victoria Melnikova

> Business and go-to-market expert for developer tools and AI. Head of New Business at Evil Martians, where she works with 40+ early-stage startups a year, and host of Dev Propulsion Labs, a podcast of face-to-face interviews with the founders behind the world's most successful developer tools.

## About
- [About Victoria Melnikova](/index.md): Bio, background, writing, talks, and podcast appearances.

## Dev Propulsion Labs (podcast)
- [All episodes](/podcast/index.md): Full episode list.
- [What Kate Holterhoff taught me about AI slop, agent experience, and the open source crisis](/podcast/kate-holterhoff-redmonk.md): July 2026 · RedMonk. From mural painting and a literature PhD to analyzing developer infrastructure. Why AI slop is drowning open source maintainers, code review as the real bottleneck, and agent experience as a new discovery channel.
- [What Matt Biilmann taught me about agent experience as the new developer experience](/podcast/matt-biilmann-netlify.md): May 2026 · Netlify. From musicology in Copenhagen to coining agent experience (AX). The four pillars of agent-ready products, and why the market for builders jumps from 17 million developers to three billion people.
- [What Max Prilutskiy taught me about going from a hackathon to YC on cold calls and word of mouth](/podcast/max-prilutskiy-lingodev.md): April 2026 · Lingo.dev. From coding at nine in Belarus to building Lingo.dev out of a hackathon with zero ideas. How cold exploration calls shaped his whole go-to-market, and why word of mouth is still the only channel that works.
- [What Amir Rustamzadeh taught me about what VCs really look for in devtools founders](/podcast/amir-rustamzadeh-firestreak.md): April 2026 · Firestreak Ventures. From JPL and SpaceX to venture capital. Why he backs founders before products, what makes a developer tool fundable, and why most developer tools never reach venture scale.
- [What Anuraag Gutgutia taught me about trust as the only currency in enterprise AI sales](/podcast/anuraag-gutgutia-truefoundry.md): March 2026 · TrueFoundry. From hedge funds to AI infrastructure, the layered trust framework for enterprise sales, surviving the year before your first big deal, and why voice agents are the next frontier.
- [What Stas Kelvich taught me about Neon's $1B acquisition and the agent gold rush](/podcast/stas-kelvich-neon.md): March 2026 · Neon. From quantum physics to co-founding a database company acquired by Databricks for $1B. Serverless Postgres, agent-driven infrastructure, and how 90 lawyers close a deal in 30 days.
- [What Harjot Gill taught me about going viral in Japan and building guardrails for AI code](/podcast/harjot-gill-coderabbit.md): February 2026 · CodeRabbit. AI code review as the new bottleneck, going viral on zen.dev, PLG meets enterprise, and why old startup playbooks are dead.
- [What David Gomes taught me about why half of developers still aren't using AI](/podcast/david-gomes-cursor.md): February 2026 · Cursor. Inside Cursor's wildly different engineering workflows, building a 50-person team in Portugal, and surviving Neon's $1B Databricks acquisition.
- [What Monica Sarbu taught me about rebuilding a product from scratch and leading with people](/podcast/monica-sarbu-xata.md): January 2026 · Xata. The question that changed Xata's product direction, rebuilding from the ground up, diverse teams winning, and people-first leadership from Romania to San Francisco.
- [What Piyush Agarwal taught me about why devtools companies don't know their customers](/podcast/piyush-agarwal-reo-dev.md): January 2026 · Reo.dev. Intent signals turning 2% conversion into 26%, GTM as the hardest unsolved problem in devtools, and building a first product as a Google Sheet.
- [What David Cramer taught me about building for the Fortune 500,000](/podcast/david-cramer-sentry.md): December 2025 · Sentry. Mass-market pricing, 4M+ developers through pure bottom-up adoption, delegating both CEO and CTO, and why 20-year-olds should stop starting companies.
- [What Abhi Aiyer taught me about making moves in AI tooling](/podcast/abhi-aiyer-mastra.md): December 2025 · Mastra. TypeScript for AI agents, the "that's cool" pivot moment, a viral book as growth engine, and raising from 120+ builder-investors.
- [What Michael Grinich taught me about building invisible infrastructure](/podcast/michael-grinich-workos.md): November 2025 · WorkOS. The plumbing behind OpenAI and Cursor, crossing the enterprise chasm, firing the marketing team, and why perfection means disappearing.
- [What Ivan Burazin taught me about walking away from revenue to bet on agents](/podcast/ivan-burazin-daytona.md): November 2025 · Daytona. Pivoting from $300K ARR on New Year's Eve, composable computers for AI agents, 19 years with the same co-founders, and genius zone go-to-market.
- [What Paul Copplestone taught me about scaling Supabase to 5M developers with no meetings](/podcast/paul-copplestone-supabase.md): November 2025 · Supabase. Hiring ex-founders with beaten-down egos, why vibe coding isn't a bubble, how Launch Weeks accidentally became a movement, and building a 30-year database company.
- [What Zeno Rocha taught me about building work so good your heroes want to copy it](/podcast/zeno-rocha-resend.md): October 2025 · Resend. $18M Series A by obsessing over every detail, 20K waitlist from founder brand, cutting scope but never quality, and the jab-jab-right-hook open source strategy.
- [What Sam Lambert taught me about databases that never go down](/podcast/sam-lambert-planetscale.md): October 2025 · PlanetScale. Surviving the AWS US-East outage, scaling from 750 to hundreds of thousands of databases, taste as competitive advantage, and vibe-coded databases.
- [What Jeff Huber taught me about context engineering and why RAG is brain rot](/podcast/jeff-huber-chroma.md): September 2025 · Chroma. Context engineering over retrieval, million-token windows changing everything, engine-vs-car open source strategy, and design as a moat in AI infrastructure.
- [What José Valim taught me about building a language from curiosity](/podcast/jose-valim-elixir.md): June 2025 · Elixir. 13 years of Elixir, choosing technical decisions over adoption trends, radical decentralization, and the future of AI tooling with Tidewave.
- [What DHH taught me about staying unconventional for 20 years](/podcast/dhh-rails.md): December 2023 · Ruby on Rails / Basecamp. 20 years of Rails, the cloud exodus, bootstrapping without VC, opinionated software, and the racing driver mindset applied to building companies.

## Elsewhere
- [Writing on the Martian Chronicles](https://evilmartians.com/martians/victoria-melnikova): Articles on devtools go-to-market, positioning, and open source.
